name: San Vito Lo Capo
description: Unwind on white sands, turquoise waters and Sicilian coastal charm
headText: Golden sands meet turquoise dreams in San Vito Lo Capo, where Sicily reveals one of its most breathtaking seaside escapes. Between dramatic cliffs and vibrant local life, every moment feels like a perfect blend of adventure, flavor, and Mediterranean magic.
locationText:
The easiest way to reach San Vito Lo Capo is by flying into nearby airports such as Palermo Airport or Trapani Airport, both about 70–100 km away. From there, you can take shuttle buses or private transfers directly to the town, or rent a car for more flexibility.
There are also regular bus connections from major cities like Palermo and Trapani, with direct or connecting routes operated by regional companies. However, there is no train station in San Vito Lo Capo, so the final part of the journey is always by bus or car.
mainText1: San Vito Lo Capo is a charming coastal town in Sicily, Italy, known for its stunning white sandy beach and crystal-clear turquoise waters. Surrounded by dramatic cliffs and nature reserves, it’s perfect for sunbathing, hiking, and enjoying fresh seafood. The town also hosts the famous Cous Cous Festival each year, attracting visitors from all over the world.
mainText2: Its narrow streets are lined with colorful buildings, quaint cafés, and artisan shops, giving it a welcoming, laid-back atmosphere. Water sports like windsurfing, kayaking, and snorkeling are popular here, while the nearby Zingaro Nature Reserve offers trails with breathtaking views and hidden coves. A paradise for both relaxation and adventure.
activitiesCarousel: The beach of San Vito Lo Capo is famous for its soft white sand and shallow, turquoise waters. With a Caribbean-like feel and views of Monte Monaco, it’s perfect for relaxing swims, sunbathing, and long, scenic seaside walks. You can rent sunbeds or use your own beach amenities and enjoy the sunshine and the refreshing sea. Behind the beach, you can freshen up in local cafés and restaurants.|https://dotonmap.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/san-vito-lo-capo-4.jpg;Macari Beach offers a quieter, more natural setting with golden sands and dramatic mountain backdrops. It’s ideal for peaceful sunsets, fewer crowds, and enjoying Sicily’s wild coastal beauty away from the main town. It is also suitable for campers and offers activities such as horseriding.|https://www.itregolfi.com/public/golfo-di-macari.jpg;Zingaro Nature Reserve is a protected coastal paradise with rugged cliffs, hiking trails, and secluded coves. It’s perfect for nature lovers, offering crystal-clear waters, rich wildlife, and some of the most breathtaking coastal scenery in Sicily. You can arrange trips to the reserve with the local travel agencies.|https://www.centrosubatlantis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/Riserva_dello_Zingaro_-_caletta.jpg
foodCarousel: Caldofreddo is a refined Sicilian dessert that perfectly balances richness and freshness. Made with layers of sponge cake, creamy ricotta, chocolate, or pistachio, it’s served chilled, almost semifreddo-style. Authentic to San Vito Lo Capo, it’s a favorite summer treat that reflects Sicily’s love for combining bold flavors with a refreshing twist.|https://www.linformazione.eu/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/caldofreddo-e1594446653226.jpg;Pasta alla Trapanese is a simple yet flavorful dish from western Sicily, made with fresh tomatoes, garlic, almonds, olive oil, and basil. The ingredients are blended into a rustic pesto and tossed with pasta, creating a fragrant, slightly nutty sauce that captures the essence of the Mediterranean.|https://cookingwithayeh.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/12/Sicilian-Pesto-Pesto-Alla-Trapanese-SQ-1.jpg;Granita is one of Sicily’s most iconic refreshments, made from finely crushed ice blended with sugar and natural flavors like lemon, almond, or coffee. Smooth yet slightly grainy, it’s traditionally enjoyed with a soft brioche bun. Perfect for hot days, it’s both a breakfast staple and a midday cool-down.|https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0024/1755/7550/files/granita_al_pistacchio_600x600.jpg?v=1627894982
goBeyondText: Take a day trip to Sicily's largest city, Palermo, and explore the vibrant culture of the old town and it's magnificent cathedrals, lively markets and delicious Italian food. For breathtaking, crystal clear beaches, embark on a day trip from San Vito Lo Capo port to the islands of Favignana.

name: Matera
description: Uncover Baroque palaces, ornate cathedral and coastal charms
headText: Golden sands meet turquoise dreams in San Vito Lo Capo, where Sicily reveals one of its most breathtaking seaside escapes. Between dramatic cliffs and vibrant local life, every moment feels like a perfect blend of adventure, flavor, and Mediterranean magic.

foodCarousel: Delicious arancini|https://steves-kitchen.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/IMG_3239.dng-Social-2.jpg;Delicious cannoli|https://www.sicilianicreativiincucina.it/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/cannoli-al-pistacchio-585x585.jpg;Delicious granita|https://cdn.shopify.com/s/files/1/0024/1755/7550/files/granita_al_pistacchio_600x600.jpg?v=1627894982

name: Ventimiglia
description: Explore seaside markets, medieval streets and Riviera charm
headText: Set along the shimmering coastline of Liguria, Ventimiglia blends Italian authenticity with a touch of French Riviera elegance. With its historic old town, lively markets, and beautiful seaside setting, Ventimiglia offers a unique mix of culture, history, and Mediterranean atmosphere.
locationText:
The easiest way to reach San Vito Lo Capo is by flying into nearby airports such as Palermo Airport or Trapani Airport, both about 70–100 km away. From there, you can take shuttle buses or private transfers directly to the town, or rent a car for more flexibility.
There are also regular bus connections from major cities like Palermo and Trapani, with direct or connecting routes operated by regional companies. However, there is no train station in San Vito Lo Capo, so the final part of the journey is always by bus or car.
mainText1: Ventimiglia offers a charming contrast between its lively modern town and its historic old quarter, perched above the Roya River. The old town is a maze of narrow alleys, stone houses, and hidden squares, where time seems to slow down. Walking through its streets reveals centuries of history, with stunning views over the sea and surrounding hills.
mainText2: The town is also famous for its vibrant markets, where locals and visitors gather to shop for fresh produce, regional specialties, and artisan goods. Along the coast, beaches and promenades provide the perfect place to relax and enjoy the Mediterranean lifestyle. With its blend of history, culture, and seaside beauty, Ventimiglia is a hidden gem of the Italian Riviera.
activitiesCarousel: Visiting the old town of Ventimiglia is like stepping into a quieter, more authentic side of the Italian Riviera. Perched above the river, this historic quarter is a maze of narrow stone alleys, archways, and hidden squares. As you wander, you’ll discover local life, small cafés, and stunning viewpoints over the sea. The atmosphere feels untouched and timeless, making it a perfect place to slow down and soak in history.|https://www.aladyinlondon.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/2Q4A3399.jpg
foodCarousel: Barbajuan is a traditional fried pastry filled with Swiss chard, ricotta, rice, and herbs. Crispy on the outside and soft inside, it’s a beloved local snack with roots in the Menton and Monaco region, often enjoyed as an appetizer or street food.|https://www.monaco-tribune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/barbagiuans-recette.jpeg
goBeyondText: Visit picturesque villages in the inlands of the Italian riviera, such as Dolceacqua, Bordighera and Seborga, with an interesting history of proclaiming itself an independent country.

name: Erice
description: Wander hilltop streets, ancient walls and panoramic coastal views
headText: Perched high above the western coast of Sicily, Erice is a breathtaking medieval town wrapped in clouds and history. With its cobbled streets, stone houses, and sweeping views over the sea, Erice feels like a timeless escape where every corner reveals a piece of Sicily’s past.
locationText: The easiest way to reach Erice is by flying into nearby airports such as Palermo Airport or Trapani Airport. From there, you can rent a car or take a bus to the town of Trapani below. A scenic cable car connects Trapani to Erice, offering stunning views during the ascent. Alternatively, you can drive up the winding mountain road for a more flexible journey.
mainText1: Erice enchants visitors with its quiet, almost mystical atmosphere. The town is a maze of narrow cobbled streets, lined with stone houses, hidden courtyards, and historic churches. As you wander through its medieval lanes, you’ll feel transported back in time, with cool mountain air and panoramic views adding to the experience.
mainText2: Despite its small size, Erice is full of charm and tradition. The town is known for its pastry shops, where local sweets reflect centuries-old recipes. Walking along its ancient walls reveals breathtaking views over the coastline and surrounding countryside. With its blend of history, scenery, and authentic Sicilian culture, Erice is a truly unforgettable destination.
activitiesCarousel: Castello di Venere is one of the most breathtaking spots in Erice, perched dramatically on the edge of a cliff overlooking the sea. Built on the ruins of an ancient temple dedicated to Venus, the castle blends mythology with medieval history. As you walk through its stone walls and gardens, you’re rewarded with sweeping panoramic views over Trapani and the coastline below. The atmosphere is peaceful, almost mystical, especially when clouds drift through the hilltop, making it a must-visit highlight.|https://st3.idealista.it/news/archivie/styles/fullwidth_xl/public/2023-07/images/erice_castelo.jpg?VersionId=1wkevjzw1SvIzUUBQpQo7PprQg7YNjb2&itok=AB3YFp2Y;Erice is home to a remarkable collection of historic churches and monasteries, reflecting its deep spiritual heritage. Among them, Chiesa Madre stands out with its imposing Gothic architecture and intricate details. As you explore the town, you’ll come across quiet chapels, cloisters, and convents tucked into narrow streets. Each tells a story of devotion and history, offering a serene contrast to the dramatic landscapes outside. Visiting these sacred spaces adds a cultural and contemplative dimension to your time in Erice.|https://bestoftrapani.altervista.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/torre-duomo-erice-scaled.jpg
foodCarousel: Frutta Martorana is one of Sicily’s most beautiful and iconic sweets. Made from almond paste, these delicacies are carefully shaped and painted to resemble real fruits with incredible detail. Originating from convent traditions, martorana fruit is as much art as it is dessert. Sweet, fragrant, and visually stunning, it’s often enjoyed during festivals but can be found year-round in pastry shops. Tasting it is a true Sicilian experience, blending craftsmanship, history, and flavor into one bite.|https://www.cibotoday.it/~media/horizontal-hi/2701078312355/la-frutta-martorana-siciliana-2-2.jpg;Lasagne alla Siciliana offers a rich and comforting twist on the classic Italian dish. Unlike the more familiar versions, this Sicilian variation often includes peas, ragù, béchamel, and sometimes boiled eggs or local cheeses layered between sheets of pasta. The peas add a subtle sweetness that perfectly balances the savory flavors, creating a unique and hearty meal. Found in traditional trattorias across Sicily, it’s a dish that reflects the island’s love for bold, layered flavors and generous portions.|https://www.mangiabedda.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/IMG_1838-2-scaled.jpg
goBeyondText: Descending from Erice to Trapani is an experience in itself, whether by scenic cable car or winding mountain road. Once below, Trapani welcomes you with a lively coastal atmosphere, historic streets, and a beautiful seafront promenade. Wander through its old town, visit local shops and cafés, or enjoy fresh seafood by the harbor. The contrast between Erice’s quiet hilltop charm and Trapani’s vibrant seaside life makes this an ideal and rewarding day trip.

name: Lindos
description: Discover whitewashed streets, ancient ruins and turquoise bays
headText: Set on the eastern coast of Rhodes, the village of Lindos is a stunning blend of ancient history and Cycladic charm. With its whitewashed houses rising beneath a dramatic acropolis, Lindos is one of the island’s most iconic and picturesque destinations.
locationText:
Land on the ancient island of Rhodes, famous for the mythological Colossus, one of the 7 Wonders of the Ancient World, a magnificent statue that, according to a legend, guarded the old town of Rhodes. The island is connected to most Greek and European airports, with more frequent flights from April to October.
Rent a car at an airport agency, or travel into the town of Rhodes, where you can take a direct bus to Lindos. Check the KTEL Rodou website to see when the buses depart, but at high season, you can expect a departure every 30 minutes.
mainText1: Lindos feels like a perfect fusion of history and seaside beauty. Its maze of narrow, cobbled streets winds through bright white houses, small courtyards, and rooftop terraces with sea views. Above it all stands the ancient Acropolis of Lindos, offering breathtaking panoramas over turquoise bays and the surrounding coastline.
mainText2: Despite its popularity, Lindos retains a relaxed and timeless atmosphere. The nearby beaches, including the calm waters of St. Paul’s Bay, are ideal for swimming and unwinding. By evening, the village transforms into a magical setting, with softly lit alleys, seaside dining, and a warm Mediterranean ambiance that lingers long after sunset.
activitiesCarousel: Acropolis of Lindos rises high above the village, offering breathtaking views of the coastline. This ancient site combines Greek, Roman, and medieval history, with ruins perched dramatically on a cliff. The climb is steep but rewarding, making it one of the most iconic landmarks on Rhodes.|https://www.friendshiptravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/Acropolis-Lindos-1024x681.jpg;Lindos Beach is a long, sandy stretch with calm, shallow waters perfect for swimming and relaxing. Located just below the village, it offers beautiful views of the acropolis above, blending history with a classic Greek beach atmosphere.|https://www.greecetravel.com/rhodes/lindos/lindos-beach.jpg;St Paul's Bay is a picturesque, sheltered cove known for its crystal-clear turquoise waters. Surrounded by rocky hills and overlooked by a small chapel, it’s one of the most scenic and peaceful swimming spots in Lindos, ideal for a relaxing escape.|https://www.greecetravelsecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/St-Pauls-Bay-Lindos-1040x675.jpg
foodCarousel: Pitaroudia are traditional fritters from Rhodes, made with chickpeas, onions, herbs, and spices. Crispy on the outside and soft inside, they’re typically served as a meze or street snack, offering a flavorful and authentic taste of the island’s local cuisine.|https://www.tasteatlas.com/images/dishes/d3b013284ade47e9bf8ae5631ac2fe04.jpg;Souvlaki is one of Greece’s most popular dishes, made with grilled pieces of meat served on skewers or in pita with vegetables and sauces. Simple, juicy, and full of flavor, it’s a must-try classic found everywhere from seaside tavernas to street food spots.|https://imageproxy.wolt.com/assets/67acaf2a7bcf8b4700f5d8b3
goBeyondText: Step into the medieval world of Rhodes Old Town, where every cobbled street tells a story. Wander through medieval alleys, explore grand castles like the Palace of the Grand Master, and discover hidden squares lined with charming cafés and boutiques. With centuries of history, vibrant culture, and stunning architecture around every corner, Rhodes Old Town is a must-visit destination for travelers seeking both adventure and timeless charm.

name: Symi
description: Admire pastel houses, elegant harbor views and timeless island charm
headText: The enchanting Greek island of Symi is known for its unique architectural style, influenced by the Italians, Ottomans and Greeks that have historically ruled over the island. Now a part of Greece, overlooking the Turkish coast, Symi is an undiscovered Aegean gem.
locationText:
Reach the island of Symi by taking a ferry from the nearest island of Rhodes. Visit the website of the Greek ferry companies, such as Blue Star Ferries, or Seajets, and check their schedule which is frequent both on and off-season.
There are many excursion boats from Rhodes taking day trips to Symi and its clear blue beaches. Symi can also be reached from other nearby Greek islands, such as Kos and Tinos, as well as from the Turkish coast city of Bodrum.
mainText1: Symi is one of the most enchanting islands in the Aegean, known for its pastel-colored neoclassical houses cascading down toward a crystal-clear harbor. The moment you arrive, the symmetry and elegance of the town feel almost unreal, like stepping into a perfectly preserved postcard. Unlike more crowded Greek destinations, Symi offers a slower, more refined atmosphere, ideal for travelers seeking beauty without the chaos.
mainText2: The island is small but full of character. Its main harbor, Gialos, is lined with waterfront tavernas where you can enjoy fresh seafood, including the island’s famous tiny shrimp. Winding staircases lead to the upper town, rewarding visitors with breathtaking views over the sea and surrounding hills. Beyond the town, Symi reveals hidden coves and quiet beaches, many accessible only by boat, with water so clear it feels almost transparent.
activitiesCarousel: A visit to the nearby Panormitis Monastery adds a cultural and spiritual dimension to the experience. Whether you spend your days swimming, exploring, or simply soaking in the atmosphere, Symi leaves a lasting impression of elegance, tranquility, and timeless Greek charm.|https://gretour.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/03/simi-monastery-of-panormitis.jpg;Visit the authentic Panormitis Sponge Shops - the island is known for sponge diving, so bring home an authentic souvenir from the depths of the turquoise Aegean sea!|https://www.goworldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Shopping-for-sponges-at-Dinos-Sponge-Center.jpg
foodCarousel: Fragosika is a type of plant, a prickly pear cactus that produces flavorful fruit that’s packed with healthy nutrients and vitamins. This plant thrives throughout the Aegean islands, and on the island of Symi, fragosika has often been dubbed the banana of the island.|https://www.tasteatlas.com/images/ingredients/260ba434a57a428fbd10ed82ceef4c87.jpg?mw=1300;A quintessential seafood delicacy of the Greek island of Symi, simiako garidaki or Symi shrimp is a unique variety of tiny shrimps that inhabit the sea around the island. Bright red, delicate, and sweet, the small crustaceans are packed with flavor, which has made them famous throughout the whole country and beyond.|https://www.tovima.gr/wp-content/uploads/2024/07/03/68240521_73171_Symi_Garidaki_ok.jpg
goBeyondText: When on Symi, don't miss the opportunity to do some island hopping on the Greek islands. You can find ferries and excursion boats to nearby islands, such as Rhodes, Chalki, Kos, as well as the volcanic island of Nisyros, which hosts an impressive active volcano.

name: Agios Prokopios
description: Relax on golden sands, crystal waters and laid-back island vibes
headText: With its tender golden sands and refreshing deep blue waters, the beach of Agios Prokopios is considered the best urban beach in all of Greece. Tucked in on the ancient island of Naxos, town of Agios Prokopios is a perfect destination for those who seek to enjoy an authentic Greek beach holiday.
locationText:
The island of Naxos is located in central Cyclades, on the shore of the Aegean sea. The island has a small airport that, during summer, offers flights from Athens and Thessaloniki, but many tourists arrive via ferries. Check the ferry companies' websites, such as Blue Star Ferries, or Seajets, and choose your preferred style of traveling.
Even though the flights are usually more pricey, they offer a quicker type of travel. Boat rides are usually longer but offer great views of the Greek islands sailing by. Once embarked on Naxos, you can rent a car and drive to your hotel in Agios Prokopios, or go for the bus option, and for just 2.30 euros reach the beach with a 15 minute ride, but be aware that during summer, buses may be packed.
mainText1: Agios Prokopios is one of the most inviting seaside spots in the Cyclades, where long stretches of golden sand meet crystal-clear, shallow waters. Just a short drive from Naxos Town, it blends natural beauty with a relaxed, easygoing atmosphere. The beach feels both lively and peaceful, offering the perfect balance between comfort and untouched charm.
mainText2: The area is well-organized yet never overwhelming. Beach bars and tavernas line the shore, serving fresh seafood and local specialties just steps from the sea. As the sun sets, the coastline takes on a warm glow, creating a laid-back evening vibe. Whether you spend your day swimming, unwinding under an umbrella, or enjoying a seaside meal, Agios Prokopios captures the essence of a perfect Greek island escape.
activitiesCarousel: Visit Naxos Chora, a charming blend of Cycladic beauty and history, where whitewashed houses climb up to a Venetian castle. Its maze-like alleys are filled with boutiques, cafés, and tavernas, while the harbor offers stunning sunset views. It’s the lively heart of the island, perfect for wandering and soaking in local culture.|https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5a3bb03b4c326d76de73ddaa/1651035197837-6V9EP93GYMEJTB4YNDGY/The+Common+Wanderer-153-2.jpg;Temple of Apollo, known as the Portara, is Naxos’ most iconic landmark. This massive marble doorway stands on a small islet, framing the sea and sky. Especially at sunset, it creates a magical scene, blending ancient history with breathtaking natural beauty.|https://www.greece-is.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/05/shutterstock_760600018.jpg;Plaka Beach is a long, sandy stretch known for its crystal-clear waters and relaxed atmosphere. Less crowded than nearby beaches, it offers plenty of space to unwind. With soft and gentle waves, it’s ideal for peaceful swims, sunbathing, and enjoying Naxos at a slower pace.|https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/5a3bb03b4c326d76de73ddaa/1648609392910-KGONAQQUNQZ66WNL2DGV/The+Common+Wanderer-156-2.jpg
foodCarousel: Naxian Cheese is a prized local specialty made from sheep and goat milk. Firm yet flavorful, it has a slightly salty, nutty taste that reflects the island’s rich pastures. Often enjoyed grilled, baked, or fresh, it’s a staple in Naxos’ traditional cuisine and a must-try for cheese lovers exploring the Cyclades.|https://www.greekgastronomyguide.gr/wp-content/uploads/2015/09/tyria-naxos-DSC_4624.jpg;Kalogeros is a sweet, nut-filled pastry unique to Naxos. Made with layers of phyllo, local honey, and almonds, it’s lightly spiced and baked to golden perfection. Traditionally served during celebrations or as a special treat, kalogeros captures the island’s authentic flavors and artisanal baking heritage.|https://www.dianekochilas.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/beef-baked-with-eggplant-and-cheese-930x620.jpg
goBeyondText: From the port of the nearby town of Agia Anna, embark on a boat tour to the neighbouring island of Paros. With its whitewashed houses and pink doors, Paros is considered as the emerging Greek island, giving Mykonos vibes with a lot less tourists, and being much more affordable. The town of Naoussa is a perfect destination for chilling by the sea and taking incredible, 'instagrammable' photos.

name: Rhodes
description: Walk through medieval history, explore emerald bays, and soak in the sun where East meets West
headText: With its imposing medieval walls and endless stretches of sun-drenched coastline, Rhodes is a captivating blend of historical grandeur and island bliss. Known as the Island of the Knights, it offers a unique journey through time, from the cobblestone streets of its UNESCO World Heritage Old Town to the shimmering turquoise waters of its world-class beaches.
locationText:
Rhodes is the largest of the Dodecanese islands, situated in the southeastern Aegean Sea near the coast of Turkey. Diagoras International Airport is well-connected year-round to Athens and serves as a major hub for seasonal direct flights from across Europe. Alternatively, the island is a primary port for large ferries departing from Piraeus, as well as high-speed catamarans connecting to nearby islands like Kos and Symi.

While the ferry ride from Athens is a scenic journey of 12 to 15 hours, many travelers prefer the quick 50-minute flight. Upon arrival, renting a car is highly recommended to explore the island's diverse landscape, from the northern tip to the southern surfing spots. There is also an extensive KTEL bus network that connects Rhodes Town to popular hubs like Lindos and Faliraki for a few euros, though schedules can vary during the peak summer heat.
mainText1: Rhodes is an island of striking contrasts, where ancient ruins sit comfortably alongside vibrant modern resorts. In the north, the medieval Old Town remains one of the best-preserved inhabited citadels in Europe, offering a labyrinth of narrow alleys and Gothic architecture. Just a short drive south, the landscape opens up to the iconic village of Lindos, where whitewashed houses cling to the hillside beneath a majestic ancient Acropolis, overlooking the serene, heart-shaped bay of St. Paul.
mainText2: Beyond the history, the island’s coastline is a playground for water lovers. On the eastern shore, beaches like Tsambika and Anthony Quinn Bay offer calm, emerald waters perfect for snorkeling and family days under the sun. Meanwhile, the southern tip at Prasonisi attracts windsurfers from around the globe. As evening falls, the island transforms; you can enjoy a sophisticated dinner in a rooftop garden in the Old Town or a relaxed 'meze' at a seaside taverna, experiencing the legendary hospitality that makes Rhodes a timeless Mediterranean treasure.
activitiesCarousel: Rising majestically over the northern side of the Old Town, the Palace of the Grand Master is a formidable example of Gothic architecture. Originally built as a Byzantine fortress and later transformed by the Knights Hospitaller, this imposing structure served as the administrative heart of the Knights' state. Walking through its massive gates, you are greeted by a grand courtyard adorned with Roman and Early Christian statues. Inside, the opulent chambers feature intricate mosaic floors brought from the island of Kos and soaring ceilings that whisper tales of medieval power. It is an essential journey into the island’s regal and turbulent past.|https://www.kastra.eu/pics/rhodes99.jpg;Stepping into the UNESCO-listed Old Town is like entering a living time capsule. The centerpiece is the Street of the Knights (Ippoton), one of the best-preserved medieval streets in Europe. Lined with the 'Inns' that once housed the different national chapters of the Order, the cobblestone path retains a solemn, noble atmosphere. Beyond this historic spine lies a labyrinth of over 200 narrow alleys where Byzantine churches, Ottoman mosques, and bustling Greek shops coexist. As you wander past hidden courtyards and ancient archways, the blend of diverse cultures creates an enchanting, timeless energy unique to Rhodes.|https://www.greece-is.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/001-palia-poli-01-.jpg;Located at the northernmost tip of Rhodes Town, Elli Beach is a vibrant mosaic of crystal-clear waters and cosmopolitan flair. Known for its iconic multi-level diving platform standing out in the sea, the beach offers a perfect blend of excitement and relaxation. You can spend your morning swimming in the refreshing Aegean or lounging on sunbeds served by trendy beach bars. With the towering Casino of Rhodes and colorful wind-swept windmills in the background, the atmosphere is electric. It is the ultimate spot for those who want the convenience of the city without sacrificing the beauty of a blue-flag beach.|https://www.rhodesguide.com/media_www/images/344916741/202112/RhodesGuide_x21q_rhodes_rown_elli_beach_aquarium_DJI_0200.jpgf;Perched on the hill of Monte Smith, the Acropolis of Rhodes offers a peaceful escape and a stunning panorama of the Aegean Sea. Unlike typical fortified citadels, this site was an expansive sanctuary dedicated to Apollo. Today, you can marvel at the partially restored Temple of Pythian Apollo, with its elegant columns reaching toward the sky. Below the temple lies the impressive 2nd-century BC stadium and a small marble theater once used for musical performances and lectures. Visiting at sunset is particularly magical, as the golden light illuminates the ancient stones, bridging the gap between modern Rhodes and its classical glory.|https://www.apolloblue.gr/app/uploads/sites/45/2020/03/Acropolis_of_Rhodes_Temple_3-1.jpg
foodCarousel: Pitaroudia are traditional fritters from Rhodes, made with chickpeas, onions, herbs, and spices. Crispy on the outside and soft inside, they’re typically served as a meze or street snack, offering a flavorful and authentic taste of the island’s local cuisine.|https://www.tasteatlas.com/images/dishes/d3b013284ade47e9bf8ae5631ac2fe04.jpg;Souvlaki is one of Greece’s most popular dishes, made with grilled pieces of meat served on skewers or in pita with vegetables and sauces. Simple, juicy, and full of flavor, it’s a must-try classic found everywhere from seaside tavernas to street food spots.|https://imageproxy.wolt.com/assets/67acaf2a7bcf8b4700f5d8b3
goBeyondText: From the port of Rhodes Town, take a ferry and visit one of the nearby islands of the Dodecanese. Whether you choose ancient island of Kos, colorful Symi, or secluded Karpathos, you are in for an amazing journey of exploration, fun and beautiful landscapes.

name: Menton
description: Stroll pastel streets, seaside promenades and citrus-scented charm
headText: A charming coastal town on the French Riviera, Menton is known for its pastel-colored buildings, lush gardens, and relaxed Mediterranean atmosphere. Nestled between the sea and the mountains near the Italian border, it offers a unique blend of French and Italian influences.
locationText:
Nearest airport to Menton is the Nice Côte d'Azur airport located in the French Riviera. You can choose to rent a car which would give you flexibility to explore the whole area in your own pace, and the other option is taking a direct, 1.5 hour train. After arriving to Nice, take the free, 2 stops long tram ride to the train station. The train runs to the Italian border town of Ventimiglia, making Menton the last stop in France.
There are two stops in Menton, one at the main station (Gare de Menton), in the western part of the town, and the other near the port (Menton Garavan). The train passes through Monaco and other towns in between. You can also reach Menton from Italy, taking a train from Genova to Ventimiglia and then, upon arrival, switching into a French train.
mainText1: With its sunny climate, scenic promenades, and citrus-scented streets, Menton is an ideal destination for a peaceful seaside escape. It is a more authentic corner of the Riviera, where pastel façades, quiet streets, and a slower pace create an almost Italian-like charm. Its history is reflected in elegant villas, lush gardens, and a beautifully preserved old town rising above the sea. The beaches, a mix of sand and pebbles, are framed by calm, clear waters and a scenic promenade.
mainText2: The beaches, a mix of sand and pebbles, are framed by calm, clear waters and a scenic promenade. The town is especially famous for the Fête du Citron, a vibrant celebration held each year, where giant sculptures made of lemons and oranges transform the city into a colorful, fragrant spectacle.
activitiesCarousel: The Plage des Sablettes beach is one of the absolute best beaches on the entire French Riviera. Old Town is the backdrop for this idyllic beach, where you can enjoy crystal clear, clean water. The beach is made up of really fine pebbles, similarly to ther French Riviera beaches. there are restaurants and shops along the promenade just behind the beach for when you need a snack or full meal.|https://cdn.sanity.io/images/nxpteyfv/goguides/e5c54c3213cafc750e76113307590a65e59accfb-1600x1067.jpg;Menton’s Old Town is absolutely picture perfect, in various shades of pinks, reds, yellows, peach, and tan, intermixed with cobblestoned streets and walls. Potted plants dot the streets and you’ll discover little archways and adorable corners tucked away all around the Old Town. Explore old Basilicas, chapels and the famous zig zag stairs connecting the old town with the beach.|https://frenchriviera.travel/wp-content/uploads/2018/07/Menton2.jpg;Besides the lemons, the colorful houses, and the fantastic interaction between the town and the sea, Menton is also known for her beautiful gardens. The Jardin Botanique Val Rahmeh is an easy 15-minute walk from Old Town and is a favorite in the city. This lovely garden was developed and cultivated starting in 1905 and holds over 1700 varieties of Mediterranean and tropical plants.|https://static.apidae-tourisme.com/filestore/objets-touristiques/images/71/179/20951879.jpg
foodCarousel: Citron de Menton is a prized local lemon known for its intense aroma, mild acidity, and naturally sweet flavor. It’s used in everything from desserts to liqueurs and savory dishes. Each year, it takes center stage during the vibrant Fête du Citron, where the town celebrates its citrus heritage with colorful parades and sculptures.|https://www.lamaisonducitron.com/cdn/shop/files/5_815834f0-2c01-482f-9265-bdae86c7bcb4.jpg?v=1767601831;Barbajuan is a traditional fried pastry filled with Swiss chard, ricotta, rice, and herbs. Crispy on the outside and soft inside, it’s a beloved local snack with roots in the Menton and Monaco region, often enjoyed as an appetizer or street food.|https://www.monaco-tribune.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/barbagiuans-recette.jpeg
goBeyondText: Get on the SNCF train and explore the small beach towns and resorts along the Riviera. You will discover many beautiful resorts on the hills overlooking the turquoise waters. Just a 10 minute ride and you will visit Monaco, one of the smallest but also wealthiest countries in Europe and the world. A luxurious tax haven, Monaco is famous for the Monaco Grand Prix, a motor racing event held annualy in late spring.

name: Èze
description: Stroll pastel streets, seaside promenades and citrus-scented charm
Climb medieval paths, panoramic views and Riviera elegance
headText: Perched high above the sparkling Mediterranean, Èze is a breathtaking hilltop village on the French Riviera that feels suspended in time. With its narrow cobbled streets, stone houses, and sweeping views over the coastline, Èze offers a perfect blend of medieval charm and dramatic natural beauty.
locationText: Èze is located between Nice and Monaco on the French Riviera. The nearest airport is Nice Côte d’Azur, about 30 minutes away by car. Renting a car offers the most flexibility, especially for exploring the scenic coastal roads. Alternatively, buses run regularly from Nice and Monaco, stopping at Èze Village below the hilltop, from where a steep but rewarding walk leads up to the old town. The nearby train station, Èze-sur-Mer, connects to the regional rail line, followed by a climb or bus ride up.
mainText1: Èze is a place to wander slowly and take in every detail. Its labyrinth of narrow streets is lined with artisan shops, small galleries, and hidden corners filled with flowers and stone archways. As you climb higher, glimpses of the deep blue Mediterranean appear between the buildings, creating unforgettable viewpoints at every turn.
mainText2: At the very top of the village, the exotic garden offers some of the most spectacular panoramic views on the Riviera. Beyond its beauty, Èze has a refined, almost mystical atmosphere, attracting artists and travelers seeking inspiration. Whether enjoying a quiet café, exploring historic pathways, or simply taking in the scenery, Èze captures the essence of timeless Riviera elegance.
activitiesCarousel: Wandering through the old town of Èze feels like stepping into a medieval dream. Narrow cobbled lanes wind between stone houses draped in flowers, leading to hidden courtyards, artisan boutiques, and quiet viewpoints overlooking the Mediterranean. The village rises steeply, so every turn reveals a new perspective—arched passages, sunlit terraces, and glimpses of the sea below. With no cars inside the historic center, the atmosphere is peaceful and timeless. Exploring Èze slowly, without a plan, is the best way to experience its charm.|https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/ce/%C3%88ze_Alpes_Maritimes_France_%28261171069%29.jpeg;Perched at the very top of Èze, the Jardin Exotique d’Èze offers some of the most spectacular views on the French Riviera. This carefully curated garden is filled with cacti, succulents, and Mediterranean plants, set among ancient ruins and sculptural installations. As you wander through its terraces, sweeping panoramas stretch from Nice to Monaco, with the deep blue sea far below. The combination of exotic flora and dramatic scenery creates a serene, almost otherworldly atmosphere high above the coast.|https://www.casadeltravel.fr/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/que-voir-a-Eze-village.jpg;The Chemin de Nietzsche is a steep and rewarding path connecting Èze-sur-Mer with the hilltop village of Èze. Named after philosopher Friedrich Nietzsche, who is said to have found inspiration here, the trail winds through rocky terrain and Mediterranean vegetation. The hike is challenging, especially in warm weather, but the views over the coastline make every step worthwhile. Whether ascending to the village or descending to the sea, it offers a unique way to experience the Riviera’s natural beauty.|https://i0.wp.com/exploretheriviera.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/Nietzsche-Path-to-Eze-18.jpg?fit=1170%2C878&ssl=1
goBeyondText: Get on the SNCF train and explore the small beach towns and resorts along the Riviera. You will discover many beautiful resorts on the hills overlooking the turquoise waters. Just a 10 minute ride and you will visit Monaco, one of the smallest but also wealthiest countries in Europe and the world. A luxurious tax haven, Monaco is famous for the Monaco Grand Prix, a motor racing event held annualy in late spring.

name: Masca
description: Explore rugged mountains, dramatic gorges and wild island beauty
headText: Tucked deep within the dramatic mountains of Tenerife, the remote village of Masca feels like a hidden world carved into the cliffs. Surrounded by rugged peaks and steep ravines, it is one of the island’s most breathtaking and mysterious destinations.
locationText:
To reach the secluded village of Masca, you must first arrive to the island of Tenerife. The lush green island is part of a large Canary archipelago, and hosts two airports - Tenerife North and Tenerife South. The North airport maintains flights only to mainland Spain, while South has flights from most European destinations, as well as some North American airports.

We recommend you to arrive at the Southern Airport, and rent a car, as it very affordable and would make your itinerary a lot more flexible. While roads in Tenerife are in very good quality, the last leg on the road to Masca can be challenging as it is very curvy, so drive carefully. Finding parking may be difficult, as the village is quite small, but worth the hassle. Other option is to take a bus to Santiago del Teide, and then take another bus that will take you to Masca, or just book a trip from a local travel agency.
mainText1: Masca is often described as one of the most spectacular villages in the Canary Islands, perched high within a deep gorge in the Teno Mountains. Its traditional stone houses cling to the hillsides, connected by narrow paths that wind through dramatic landscapes. The journey to reach Masca, along a narrow, winding road, is an experience in itself, offering unforgettable panoramic views at every turn.
mainText2: Despite its small size, Masca has a powerful presence. The surrounding valley, filled with palm trees and rugged terrain, creates a sense of isolation and tranquility. From here, adventurous visitors can hike through the famous Masca Gorge down to the ocean, passing towering cliffs and wild scenery. It’s a place where nature feels raw and untouched, offering a completely different side of Tenerife.
activitiesCarousel: Wander the tiny, atmospheric village where stone houses cling to steep hillsides. Narrow paths wind between terraces, palm trees, and viewpoints, revealing stunning vistas at every turn. Walking through Masca feels like stepping back in time, offering a peaceful escape into one of Tenerife’s most authentic and remote settings.|https://www.tenerifeaz.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/tenerifeaz-masca-visite-scaled-e1676621604235-1024x683.jpeg;The hike through Masca Gorge is one of the island’s most iconic adventures. Descending through a dramatic ravine, the trail passes towering cliffs and rugged terrain before reaching the ocean. It’s a challenging but rewarding experience, perfect for nature lovers seeking unforgettable scenery.|https://tabaibaguesthouse.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/Hike-to-Masca.jpg;The viewpoints around Masca offer some of the most breathtaking panoramas in Tenerife. Overlooking deep valleys and jagged peaks, they’re ideal for photos and quiet moments. Sunrise and sunset are especially magical, as the light transforms the landscape into a dramatic, ever-changing scene.|https://outofofficemindset.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/Masca-Flowers-Tenerife-Canary-Islands.jpg
foodCarousel: Enjoy the sweet taste of Canary bananas while taking in the dramatic views of Masca. Smaller and richer in flavor than typical bananas, they’re a local specialty. Paired with Masca’s rugged cliffs and deep valleys, this simple moment becomes a uniquely authentic Tenerife experience. You can find a fruit stand on the path to the Masca rock.|https://tenerifeconnect.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/1-12-1068x698-1-e1726481388545.png;Cabrito en Salsa is a traditional dish from the Canary Islands, especially in rural areas like Tenerife villages. Slow-cooked until tender, the goat meat is simmered with garlic, wine, herbs, and spices, creating a rich, hearty flavor. It reflects the island’s rustic culinary roots and is often enjoyed after a day of exploring the mountains.|https://volcanicxperience.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/carne-de-cabra.jpg;Tarta de Santiago is a classic dessert made with ground almonds, eggs, and sugar, giving it a moist, rich texture and delicate flavor. Often dusted with powdered sugar and marked with a cross, it’s a simple yet elegant sweet treat, perfect to enjoy overlooking the dramatic landscapes of Masca.|https://www.marcellinaincucina.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/spanish-almond-cake-featured.jpg
goBeyondText: Explore the northwestern edge of the island of Tenerife. From Masca, drive to an old town Santiago del Teide and explore the authentic church. Continue to the north, to the Upper Teno Moonscape, an incredible out-of-this-world hiking trail, one of many across the island. Then, drive to the charming coastal town of Garachico and grab a snack to round up a perfect day trip from Masca.

name: Deià
description: Experience stone villages, artistic soul and breathtaking sea views
headText: Perched on the rugged northwest coast of Mallorca, the village of Deià has long attracted artists, writers, and travelers seeking inspiration. With its stone houses, terraced gardens, and breathtaking mountain views, Deià is a hidden Balearic gem where creativity and natural beauty come together.
locationText:
To get to Deià, reach the sunny island of Mallorca from any larger Spanish or European airport, with more frequent departures, but also higher prices, during summer season. It is best advised to rent a car in Mallorca, as the island is relatively large and offers great destinations which need flexibility if you want to truly enjoy them and spend more time.

If you're not fond of driving, you can take the bus from the main bus station in Palma. Take a 15 minute bus from the airport to the bus station (bus A11), and then take the bus 203 to Deià. The ride lasts for about 50 minutes and costs about 3 euros per person, one way.
mainText1: Deià exudes charm from the moment you arrive. Narrow cobblestone streets wind past rustic stone houses adorned with vibrant shutters and flowering balconies. The village’s serene atmosphere makes it feel like stepping into a timeless postcard. Perched above the Mediterranean, every corner offers stunning vistas of the turquoise sea and dramatic Tramuntana Mountains. Unlike busier Mallorca towns, Deià feels intimate and refined, perfect for a peaceful escape.
mainText2: Though small, Deià is full of character. Cozy cafés, local boutiques, and artisan galleries invite exploration, while hiking trails in the surrounding mountains reward visitors with sweeping views. The village is also famed for its literary and artistic history, having inspired writers like Robert Graves. Whether strolling through its streets or enjoying a quiet meal with a sea view, Deià leaves a lasting impression of beauty, culture, and tranquility.
activitiesCarousel: Spend some time swimming in Cala Deià. It is a small, picturesque cove with crystal-clear waters and a pebble beach tucked beneath dramatic cliffs. Surrounded by pine trees and traditional stone houses, it’s perfect for swimming, snorkeling, or simply relaxing while enjoying stunning views of the Mediterranean. The quiet charm makes it a favorite spot for both locals and visitors seeking tranquility.|https://abcmallorcastorage.blob.core.windows.net/images/2018/01/cala-deia-hike-10-copy.jpg;Wandering through Deià’s old town will make you feel like stepping back in time. Narrow cobblestone streets wind past charming stone houses, vibrant shutters, and quaint courtyards. Visitors can explore artisan shops, art galleries, and cozy cafés, while soaking in mountain and sea views at every turn, making it an ideal destination for leisurely strolls and cultural discovery.|https://www.helencummins.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/deia-guide-streets-01.jpg
foodCarousel: Your trip to Mallorca is not complete without trying an ensaïmada. Ensaïmada is a soft, spiraled pastry, light and airy with a delicate sweetness. Traditionally dusted with powdered sugar, it can be filled with cream, pumpkin jam, or chocolate. Perfect for breakfast or a midday treat, ensaïmada is a quintessential taste of the island, beloved by locals and visitors alike.|https://cdn.tasteatlas.com/images/dishes/48612786ce2f4c00ab65c08f43a69f86.jpg;Mallorcan Paella is a flavorful twist on the classic Spanish dish, often featuring local ingredients like seafood, pork, chicken, and seasonal vegetables. Cooked slowly over an open flame, it’s rich, aromatic, and deeply satisfying. Served in traditional restaurants and seaside eateries, Mallorcan paella is a must-try culinary experience that captures the island’s authentic flavors.|https://www.estilopalma.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Paella-Es-Canyis-5148.jpg
goBeyondText: Deià is not the only secluded gem in Mallorca, the other being the neighobouring village of Valldemossa. Separated from Deià by a mountain range, Valldemossa is a cute Mallorcan village with beautiful old streets and lively shops.

name: San Cristóbal de La Laguna
description: Stroll colonial streets, colorful façades and vibrant local culture
headText: Rich in history and vibrant local life, San Cristóbal de La Laguna is Tenerife’s cultural heart. This UNESCO-listed city is known for its colorful colonial architecture, lively atmosphere, and authentic Canarian charm, offering a completely different experience from the island’s coastal resorts.
locationText: San Cristóbal de La Laguna is located in the northern part of Tenerife, just a few minutes from Tenerife North Airport, making it one of the easiest destinations to reach on the island. It is well connected by tram and bus to nearby cities like Santa Cruz. Renting a car is optional here, as public transport is reliable and convenient for exploring both the city and its surroundings.
mainText1: La Laguna enchants visitors with its elegant streets and colorful façades. Its grid-like layout, rare for its time, inspired many colonial cities in Latin America. As you wander through its historic center, you’ll find beautifully preserved buildings, charming courtyards, and a lively mix of shops, cafés, and local culture at every corner.
mainText2: Beyond its architecture, La Laguna is full of life. As a university city, it has a youthful and energetic atmosphere, with vibrant plazas and a thriving food scene. Museums, churches, and cultural events add depth to the experience, making it a perfect destination for those seeking history, authenticity, and a taste of everyday life in Tenerife.
activitiesCarousel: Iglesia de la Concepción Tower is one of the most iconic landmarks in the city and offers one of the best viewpoints in northern Tenerife. Climbing to the top rewards visitors with panoramic views over La Laguna’s colorful streets, nearby mountains, and even the Atlantic on clear days. The tower itself is rich in history, being part of one of the oldest churches on the island. Visiting it gives you both a cultural insight and a stunning visual perspective of this UNESCO-listed city.|https://photos.smugmug.com/Canarias-Lovers/Tenerife/San-Cristobal-de-La-Laguna/Torre-de-la-Concepcion/i-sBDtvKw/0/MqVrxXR8zhCRm95JNRtnvDGLZfr9bbwX4CBLL5MrG/L/downstairs-torre-concepcion-la-laguna-tenerife-L.jpg;Anaga Rural Park is a misty, mystical landscape of ancient laurel forests, rugged peaks, and winding trails. Often wrapped in clouds, it feels untouched and wild. It’s perfect for scenic drives, hikes, and discovering a lush, green side of Tenerife that feels worlds away from the coast.|https://top-car-hire.com/uploads/images/coche-alquiler-parque-rural-anaga.jpg
foodCarousel: Arepas are a must-try when visiting northern Tenerife. These cornmeal pockets, typically filled with cheese, meat, or avocado, are simple, flavorful, and incredibly satisfying. Venezuelan street food is especially common here due to strong historical and migration ties between the Canary Islands and Venezuela. Many Canarians emigrated to Venezuela in the past and later returned, bringing culinary traditions with them. Today, this cultural exchange is reflected in the local food scene, making arepas a staple you’ll find across La Laguna.|https://queeniesdailysnippets.wordpress.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/330468766_909067336889945_6477096556605955117_n.jpg;If you're up for a snack, try pimientos de Padrón, small green peppers blistered in olive oil and sprinkled with sea salt. Mild and slightly smoky, they’re a popular tapas across Spain—but with a twist: occasionally, one is surprisingly spicy. This element of chance makes them a fun and authentic snack to enjoy with a drink.|https://vegantravellife.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/06/pimientos-de-padron.jpg
goBeyondText: Drive to Mirador Los 500 escalones, one of the most amazing viewpoints in all of Tenerife. Enjoy peaceful and colorful sunset overlooking the powerful Atlantic ocean.

name: Formentera
description: Unwind on turquoise shores, hidden coves and laid-back island vibes
headText: Just south of Ibiza, the island of Formentera feels like a Mediterranean dream. Known for its crystal-clear waters, white sandy beaches, and relaxed atmosphere, Formentera offers a more peaceful and untouched escape compared to its lively neighbor.
locationText: Formentera has no airport, so the easiest way to reach it is by ferry from Ibiza, with regular departures taking about 30–60 minutes. Ibiza itself is well connected to major European cities by air. Once on the island, renting a scooter, bike, or car is the best way to explore its beaches and hidden corners at your own pace.
mainText1: Formentera is all about simplicity and natural beauty. Its beaches are among the clearest in Europe, with shallow turquoise waters and soft white sand stretching for miles. The island’s flat landscape makes it perfect for cycling, allowing you to move easily between beaches, coves, and quiet villages.
mainText2: Despite its small size, Formentera offers a unique charm. Rustic beach bars, fresh seafood restaurants, and peaceful sunsets create a relaxed, almost bohemian atmosphere. Whether you’re swimming in crystal-clear waters or enjoying a slow evening by the sea, Formentera invites you to unwind and experience the Mediterranean at its purest.
activitiesCarousel: Platja de Ses Illetes is often ranked among the most beautiful beaches in Europe. Stretching along a narrow peninsula, it offers crystal-clear turquoise waters on both sides, creating a surreal, almost Caribbean-like setting. The shallow sea is perfect for swimming and relaxing, while soft white sand invites long, peaceful walks. With views reaching toward Ibiza, this beach is the ultimate highlight of Formentera and a must-visit.|https://formenteralifestyle.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/IMG_1403.jpg;Es Pujols Beach combines natural beauty with a lively yet relaxed atmosphere. Located near the island’s main resort area, it offers clear, calm waters ideal for swimming and sunbathing. Small rocky coves and sandy stretches create variety, while nearby restaurants and cafés make it easy to spend the whole day here. It’s the perfect spot for those who want both beach tranquility and a touch of local life.|https://www.formentera.es/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/1-shutterstock_1370es-pujols-55761.jpg;Sant Francesc Xavier is the charming capital of the island and a great place to experience authentic local life. Centered around a whitewashed church that once served as a fortress, the town features small boutiques, artisan shops, and cozy cafés. Its laid-back atmosphere invites you to slow down, wander the streets, and enjoy a quieter side of Formentera beyond the beaches.|https://images.trvl-media.com/place/6221090/b2cb470a-267f-4683-9849-e2021e39b8db.jpg
foodCarousel: Sobrassada is a traditional cured sausage from the Balearic Islands, known for its soft texture and rich, smoky flavor. Made with pork, paprika, and spices, it’s often spread on bread or served with local cheese. In Formentera, enjoying sobrassada with a glass of wine by the sea is a simple yet authentic culinary experience that reflects the island’s Mediterranean roots.|https://canpistraques.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/sobrassada_bodego_B.jpg;Mallorcan Paella is a flavorful twist on the classic Spanish dish, often featuring local ingredients like seafood, pork, chicken, and seasonal vegetables. Cooked slowly over an open flame, it’s rich, aromatic, and deeply satisfying. Served in traditional restaurants and seaside eateries, Mallorcan paella is a must-try culinary experience that captures the island’s authentic flavors.|https://www.estilopalma.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Paella-Es-Canyis-5148.jpg
goBeyondText: A trip from Formentera to Ibiza is quick and easy, with regular ferries taking under an hour. While Formentera offers peace and relaxation, Ibiza brings a livelier atmosphere, with historic sites like Dalt Vila, vibrant markets, and famous nightlife. Whether you visit for culture, shopping, or a change of pace, combining both islands gives you the best of the Balearic experience.

name: Taganana
description: Wander lush mountains, remote villages and untamed coastal beauty
headText: Hidden within the lush mountains of Tenerife, the remote village of Taganana feels like a step into a wilder, untouched side of the island. Surrounded by dramatic peaks and the Atlantic Ocean, it is one of Tenerife’s most authentic and scenic destinations.
locationText:
Taganana is located within the Anaga Rural Park, on the northeast shores of Tenerife. Fly into Tenerife North airport, which is connected with the largest airports in mainland Spain, as well as some South American airports.
Your best option is to rent a car, which is considered very affordable for Tenerife, and explore the surroundings with much flexibility, or book a guided tour. If you take a bus, get to the capital city of Santa Cruz and take a bus 946 which will take you to Taganana. Check Titsa Bus company website for more info on buses and departure times.
mainText1: Taganana is a place where nature takes center stage. Nestled within the Anaga Rural Park, the village is surrounded by steep green mountains, winding roads, and breathtaking viewpoints. Traditional houses are scattered across the hillsides, creating a raw and unspoiled atmosphere. The journey to reach Taganana is just as memorable, with panoramic views that reveal a completely different side of Tenerife.
mainText2: Despite its remote feel, Taganana is rich in local life and tradition. Nearby black-sand beaches like Playa de Benijo offer dramatic coastal scenery, perfect for sunsets and quiet walks. Small family-run restaurants serve fresh seafood and Canarian specialties, adding to the authentic charm. Taganana is a place to slow down, connect with nature, and experience Tenerife beyond the typical tourist path.
activitiesCarousel: Anaga Rural Park is a misty, mystical landscape of ancient laurel forests, rugged peaks, and winding trails. Often wrapped in clouds, it feels untouched and wild. It’s perfect for scenic drives, hikes, and discovering a lush, green side of Tenerife that feels worlds away from the coast.|https://top-car-hire.com/uploads/images/coche-alquiler-parque-rural-anaga.jpg;Wandering through Taganana reveals a quiet, traditional village tucked into the mountains. Narrow roads, hillside homes, and local eateries create an authentic atmosphere. Take your time to explore viewpoints, enjoy local cuisine, and soak in the peaceful rhythm of life far from the busy tourist areas.|https://thumbs.dreamstime.com/b/taganana-village-anaga-rural-park-tenerife-island-small-treet-canary-islands-89567277.jpg;Playa de Benijo is a dramatic black-sand beach framed by jagged rock formations and powerful Atlantic waves. It’s one of the best sunset spots on the island, where golden light meets dark volcanic sand. Perfect for a quiet escape, it offers raw natural beauty and unforgettable coastal views.|https://images.mnstatic.com/b7/d1/b7d13bf79e1ecae61228b602bafbeabf.jpg
foodCarousel: Gambas al Ajillo is a classic Spanish tapas dish made with shrimp sautéed in olive oil, garlic, and chili. Served sizzling hot, it’s rich, aromatic, and perfect for sharing. Simple yet full of flavor, it’s a must-try in coastal destinations, especially when paired with fresh bread to soak up the sauce.|https://images.immediate.co.uk/production/volatile/sites/30/2025/06/GambasPilPil-0a9531b.jpg;If you're up for a snack, try pimientos de Padrón, small green peppers blistered in olive oil and sprinkled with sea salt. Mild and slightly smoky, they’re a popular tapas across Spain—but with a twist: occasionally, one is surprisingly spicy. This element of chance makes them a fun and authentic snack to enjoy with a drink.|https://vegantravellife.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/06/pimientos-de-padron.jpg
goBeyondText: Drive along the northeast coast of Tenerife, and explore many of the mindblowing landscapes, dramatic, lush mountains crashing into dark blue ocean. Enjoy the relaxing scenery of the Roque de las Bodegas beach, another must-see sunset spot.

name: Ayia Napa
description: Experience golden beaches, vibrant nightlife and crystal-clear Mediterranean waters
headText: Set along the southeastern coast of Cyprus, Ayia Napa is a lively seaside destination known for its turquoise waters, sun-drenched beaches, and energetic atmosphere. Once a quiet fishing village, it has transformed into one of the Mediterranean’s most popular holiday spots.
locationText: Ayia Napa is located on the eastern coast of Cyprus, about 40 minutes from Larnaca International Airport, the island’s main gateway. The easiest way to reach Ayia Napa is by car or shuttle transfer from the airport. Buses also run regularly between Larnaca, Paralimni, and Ayia Napa, making it an accessible destination without a car, especially during the summer season.
mainText1: Ayia Napa is all about the sea. Its coastline is dotted with some of Cyprus’s most beautiful beaches, including Nissi Beach and Makronissos, known for soft sand and incredibly clear, shallow waters. Days are spent swimming, sunbathing, or enjoying water sports, while boat trips reveal hidden coves and sea caves along the coast.
mainText2: Beyond the beaches, Ayia Napa offers a mix of culture and nightlife. The historic monastery at the town’s center provides a glimpse into its past, while the evenings transform the town into a vibrant hub of music, bars, and clubs. Whether you’re seeking relaxation by the sea or lively nights out, Ayia Napa delivers a dynamic Mediterranean escape.
activitiesCarousel: Nissi Beach is the most famous beach in Ayia Napa, known for its powdery white sand and shallow, crystal-clear waters. A small islet just offshore can be reached by wading through the sea, giving the beach its name. With beach bars, music, and water sports, it has a lively, youthful atmosphere, especially in summer. Despite its popularity, the natural beauty of the turquoise sea makes it an unforgettable spot for both relaxation and fun.|https://cyprus-mail.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/nissi2.jpg;Makronissos Beach offers a more relaxed alternative to the busier beaches nearby. Known for its soft golden sand and calm, clear waters, it’s perfect for swimming and unwinding. The beach is actually a collection of small bays, giving visitors more space and a quieter feel. Nearby, you’ll also find ancient tombs carved into the rock, adding a subtle historical touch to this serene coastal setting.|https://www.swedishnomad.com/wp-content/images/2019/01/Makronissos-beach.jpg;Cape Greco is a protected natural park between Ayia Napa and Protaras, famous for its dramatic cliffs and panoramic sea views. It’s a paradise for nature lovers, with hiking and cycling trails winding through rugged landscapes. The area is also known for its sea caves and crystal-clear waters, ideal for snorkeling or cliff diving. Sunrises and sunsets here are particularly stunning, offering a peaceful escape from the lively resort atmosphere.|https://chooseyourcyprus.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/SEA-CAVES1.jpg
foodCarousel: Kleftiko is a typical Greek and Cypriot dish that is commonly prepared at festive events and celebrations, consisting of a succulent piece of lamb or goat meat that is wrapped in foil and baked in airtight ovens. Also spelled as klephtiko, the name comes from the Klephts, mountain rebels from the Greek Revolution who cooked the food underground, so that no aromas or steam could escape, as it could reveal their positions.|https://www.sainsburysmagazine.co.uk/uploads/media/1800x1800/07/14557-lamb-kleftiko.jpg?v=1-0;Halloumi is one of Cyprus’s most iconic foods, known for its firm texture and salty, slightly tangy flavor. Traditionally made from a mixture of sheep’s and goat’s milk, it has a high melting point, making it perfect for grilling or frying. Served warm, it develops a crispy golden crust while remaining soft inside. Whether enjoyed on its own, in salads, or alongside fresh bread, halloumi is a must-try taste of Cypriot cuisine.|https://cdn.loveandlemons.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/halloumi-500x500.jpg
goBeyondText: Varosha is a haunting and thought-provoking experience. Once a thriving resort area, it was abandoned in 1974 and remained frozen in time for decades. Today, parts of it can be visited, revealing empty hotels, silent streets, and a surreal atmosphere along the coastline. Walking or cycling through Varosha offers a rare glimpse into recent history, making it a unique and reflective contrast to the lively beaches of nearby Ayia Napa.

name: Óbidos
description: Step into medieval streets, castle walls and storybook charm
headText: Nestled within medieval walls and cobbled streets, Óbidos is a picture-perfect town that feels frozen in time. Famous for its whitewashed houses, colorful doors, and historic charm, Óbidos is one of Portugal’s most enchanting and storybook-like destinations.
locationText:
You can arrive to Óbidos via direct bus line from Lisbon. Take the yellow metro line to Campo Grande station in northern Lisbon, next to the bus station. The bus ride lasts for about one hour and costs around 8 euros per person, one way.

The bus leaves a few times during the day. Check Rodoviaria do Oeste website of the bus company for exact departure times. The other option to reach Óbidos is to rent a car in Lisbon, or Lisbon airport upon arrival.
mainText1: Óbidos captivates visitors from the moment they enter through its ancient gates. The narrow streets are lined with charming shops, cafés, and artisanal boutiques, all framed by vibrant flowers and quaint façades. Wandering through the town feels like stepping into a fairy tale, with every corner revealing a glimpse of its rich history and medieval heritage.
mainText2: The town is small but full of character. Walking along the old castle walls offers sweeping views over red-tiled rooftops and surrounding countryside. Óbidos is also famous for its traditional treats, including the sweet cherry liqueur, Ginjinha, served in chocolate cups. Throughout the year, festivals and cultural events bring the streets to life, making Óbidos a timeless destination for travelers seeking charm, history, and a touch of magic.
activitiesCarousel: Explore Óbidos Castle, a striking medieval fortress that dominates the town skyline. Its imposing walls, towers, and battlements date back centuries, offering visitors panoramic views of the red-tiled rooftops and surrounding countryside. A walk along the castle grounds provides a glimpse into Portugal’s rich history and the town’s strategic importance during the Middle Ages.|https://media.istockphoto.com/id/471571001/photo/obidos-castle.jpg?s=612x612&w=0&k=20&c=EfUrFBOCHWRxU0KeVecuf0h9q_BoXqd25SNgn-d5krM=;St Mary Church is a beautifully preserved 12th-century church located in the heart of Óbidos. Known for its elegant white façade, ornate interiors, and religious artworks, it reflects the town’s historical and cultural heritage. Visitors often admire its tranquil atmosphere and intricate details, making it a highlight of any stroll through the medieval streets.|https://turismo.obidos.pt/site/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/s_maria_ext.jpg
foodCarousel: Ginjinha de Óbidos is a beloved Portuguese cherry liqueur, and in Óbidos it reaches a sweet, indulgent peak when served in edible chocolate cups. Rich, slightly tart, and wonderfully aromatic, it’s a must-try local treat that combines traditional flavor with a playful, decadent twist—perfect for sipping as you wander the town’s cobbled streets.|https://www.obidosportugaltourism.com/images/ginja-de-obidos.jpg;Bifana is a classic Portuguese street food made with tender, marinated pork served in a soft roll. Savory, lightly spiced, and juicy, it’s a quick and satisfying snack that embodies the flavors of Portugal. Found in cafés, markets, and festival stalls, the bifana is an essential culinary experience when exploring Óbidos or any Portuguese town.|https://images.slurrp.com/prod/recipe_images/leites-culinaria/recipes-bifanas-portuguese-pork-sandwiches.html-1646671181_HT7PX3H5OQMTP0GJKH7A.webp
goBeyondText: Visit the neighbouring town of Caldas da Rainha with its late gothic church Igreja de Nossa Senhora do Pópulo. The church was declared a national monument of Portugal in 1910.

name: Sintra
description: Wander fairytale palaces, lush hills and misty romantic landscapes
headText: Nestled among forested hills just outside Lisbon, Sintra is a magical destination known for its colorful palaces, misty landscapes, and romantic charm. With its unique blend of nature and architecture, Sintra feels like a real-life fairy tale, making it one of Portugal’s most enchanting and unforgettable places.
locationText:
Arrive to the capital of Portugal, Lisbon, and take a metro ride to the Rossio station, at the Lisbon train station. From there, take a CP Train to Sintra. The train ride costs around 2-3 euros per person, per way. Check CP website for exact information on train departures.

To get to Sintra's castles, take the bus 434 from the town's bus stops. The bus stops at all 3 castles, and day ticket costs around 11-12 euros per person.
mainText1: Sintra captivates visitors with its dreamlike atmosphere. Winding roads lead through dense forests to extraordinary landmarks like the vibrant Pena Palace and the mysterious Quinta da Regaleira. The town itself is filled with charming streets, historic villas, and hidden gardens, where every corner feels steeped in history, legend, and imagination.
mainText2: Despite its popularity, Sintra retains a sense of mystery and tranquility. The surrounding hills are perfect for exploration, offering scenic viewpoints and peaceful escapes into nature. Whether you’re wandering through palace grounds or enjoying local pastries in a quiet café, Sintra offers a magical blend of culture, nature, and timeless beauty.
activitiesCarousel: Pena Palace is one of Europe’s most iconic fairytale castles, perched high above Sintra’s hills. Its vibrant colors, romantic architecture, and panoramic views make it unforgettable. Surrounded by lush gardens and hidden paths, the palace feels like a dream brought to life, offering both history and breathtaking scenery.|https://builder.livingtours.com/public/images/produtos/sintra%20private%20tour%20half%20day.jpg;Quinta da Regaleira is a mysterious estate filled with hidden tunnels, symbolic architecture, and enchanting gardens. Its most famous feature, the Initiation Well, is a spiraling underground tower that feels both mystical and surreal. Exploring the grounds is like uncovering secrets from another world.|https://www.grownuptravels.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/07/Quinta-da-Regaleira-Initiation-well.jpg;Cabo da Roca marks the westernmost point of mainland Europe, where dramatic cliffs plunge into the Atlantic Ocean. The views are wild and powerful, especially at sunset when the sky meets the endless sea. It’s a place that feels raw and untamed, perfect for experiencing nature at its most striking.|https://www.islands.com/img/gallery/mainland-europes-westernmost-point-is-a-gorgeous-portugal-park-with-dramatic-cliffs-and-stunning-views/l-intro-1756810890.jpg
foodCarousel: Travesseiros are soft, pillow-shaped pastries from Sintra, filled with a sweet almond and egg cream. Their flaky, buttery layers and delicate sweetness make them a perfect breakfast treat or afternoon indulgence, often enjoyed with a coffee while strolling through the town’s charming streets.|https://www.pingodoce.pt/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/travesseiros.jpg;Queijadas are traditional small cheese tarts, subtly sweet and slightly tangy, made with fresh cheese, sugar, and cinnamon. Crispy on the outside and creamy inside, they are a beloved local specialty that captures the authentic flavors of Sintra.|https://assets.tmecosys.com/image/upload/t_web_rdp_recipe_584x480/img/recipe/ras/Assets/9DC6ABEA-6480-41B3-A53E-173F7A9CA0B3/Derivates/CC07F370-A7A0-4DFF-A8A5-FE2891A1A1FE.jpg
goBeyondText: Azenhas do Mar is a stunning cliffside village just a short drive from Sintra, offering breathtaking views of the Atlantic Ocean. Whitewashed houses cascade down toward the rocky shore, framing a natural seawater pool perfect for a refreshing dip. Strolling along its narrow streets, you’ll find cozy cafés and traditional restaurants serving fresh seafood. The dramatic cliffs, crashing waves, and peaceful village charm make Azenhas do Mar a perfect day-trip escape from Sintra.

name: Hallstatt
description: Discover alpine lakes, pastel houses and postcard-perfect views
headText: Tucked between towering mountains and the serene waters of Hallstätter See, Hallstatt is one of Europe’s most breathtaking lakeside villages. Known for its pastel houses, dramatic alpine setting, and rich history, Hallstatt feels like a place straight out of a postcard, offering a perfect blend of nature and timeless charm.
locationText:
Reaching Hallstatt from Salzburg is both easy and scenic, with several great options. The most popular is the train: take a train from Salzburg to Attnang-Puchheim, then continue to Hallstatt station. From there, a short ferry ride across the lake brings you directly into the village—one of the most beautiful arrivals in Europe.

Alternatively, driving takes about 1.5 hours and offers stunning alpine views along the way. For a more relaxed option, guided tours and buses run regularly, providing a hassle-free way to experience Hallstatt as a day trip from Salzburg.
mainText1: Hallstatt enchants visitors with its peaceful beauty and picture-perfect scenery. Narrow streets wind between colorful houses and flower-filled balconies, all set against the backdrop of towering mountains. The lakeside promenade offers stunning reflections of the village in the water, creating one of the most iconic views in Europe.
mainText2: Though small, Hallstatt is full of unique experiences. From exploring its historic salt mines to taking in panoramic views from the Skywalk, the village offers both adventure and tranquility. Cozy cafés and local shops add to its charm, making Hallstatt a destination where nature, history, and beauty come together in perfect harmony.
activitiesCarousel: Hallstätter See offers one of the most peaceful ways to experience Hallstatt—by pedal boat. Gliding across the calm, mirror-like water, you’ll be surrounded by alpine peaks and postcard views of Hallstatt. Swans often drift nearby, adding to the fairytale atmosphere. It’s a relaxing, unforgettable way to take in the village from a unique and serene perspective.|https://www.hallstatt.net/assets/Hallstatt/Artikel/ueber-hallstatt/sehenswertes/hallstaettersee/bootsverleih-krumboeck/_resampled/WebsitePictureW10/hallstatt-bootsverleih-riedler-krumboeck-c-fallnhauser-10.jpg;For some spooks, visit the Hallstatt Lutheran Church. The church stands above the village with its iconic tower overlooking the lake. Nearby, the unusual Hallstatt Charnel House holds painted skulls, carefully decorated with names and floral motifs. This centuries-old tradition reflects local customs and limited burial space, offering a fascinating and slightly eerie glimpse into Hallstatt’s cultural history.|https://www.hallstatt.net/assets/image-gallery/beinhaus-hallstatt/beinhaus-hallstatt-neue-bildergalerie/_resampled/CroppedFocusedImageWzgwMCw0NTAsInkiLDQxXQ/2018-03-beinhaus-hallstatt-c-krauss-hallstatt-net-0008-5208.jpg;Take the cable car up to the heights above Hallstatt for breathtaking panoramic views of the lake and mountains. At the top, you’ll find the famous Skywalk and access to the Hallstatt Salt Mine, one of the oldest in the world. Exploring its tunnels and slides reveals the region’s deep mining heritage, making it both an exciting and educational experience.|https://www.mewithmysuitcase.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/Salt-Mine.jpg
goBeyondText: Perched high above the Alps, the 5 Fingers Observation Deck offers one of the most thrilling viewpoints near Hallstatt. Extending like a hand over a sheer drop, each platform provides a unique perspective of the surrounding mountains and lakes. The views are truly breathtaking, especially on clear days, making it a must-visit for those seeking unforgettable scenery and a touch of adventure.

name: St. Wolfgang
description: Discover alpine lakes, storybook villages and timeless Austrian charm
headText: Nestled along the shores of Lake Wolfgang and surrounded by the rolling peaks of the Salzkammergut region, St. Wolfgang is a picture-perfect Austrian village that blends natural beauty with historic elegance. With its colorful facades, lakeside promenades, and iconic church, it feels like a serene escape into a classic alpine postcard.
locationText: St. Wolfgang is located in the Salzkammergut region of Upper Austria, about one hour from Salzburg. The easiest way to reach it is by car, offering scenic drives through lakes and mountains. Alternatively, you can take a bus from Salzburg to nearby towns like Bad Ischl and continue onward. During warmer months, boat connections across Lake Wolfgang also provide a beautiful and relaxing way to arrive.
mainText1: St. Wolfgang is defined by its lakeside setting and peaceful atmosphere. The clear waters of Lake Wolfgang reflect the surrounding mountains, while the village’s charming streets are lined with traditional houses, cafés, and small shops. Visitors can stroll along the promenade, take a boat ride, or simply relax and enjoy the calm rhythm of alpine life.
mainText2: Despite its tranquil feel, St. Wolfgang offers plenty to explore. The historic parish church with its famous winged altar stands at the heart of the town, while the Schafberg railway takes visitors up to breathtaking panoramic views over the region. Whether enjoying local Austrian cuisine, exploring nearby hiking trails, or cruising the lake, St. Wolfgang captures the essence of Austria’s most scenic landscapes.
activitiesCarousel: A cruise on Lake Wolfgang is one of the most relaxing and scenic ways to experience the beauty of the Salzkammergut region. Traditional boats glide across the clear, emerald waters, connecting charming lakeside towns like St. Wolfgang im Salzkammergut, St. Gilgen, and Strobl. As you drift along, panoramic views of rolling green hills and alpine peaks unfold in every direction. Whether you choose a short ride or a longer journey, the peaceful atmosphere and postcard-perfect scenery make it an unforgettable highlight of any visit.|https://www.salzburg.info/deskline/infrastruktur/objekte/SchafbergBahn%20%26%20WolfgangseeSchifffahrt_14529/1222680/image-thumb__1222680__slider-main/schifffahrt-am-wolfgangsee_38304329.f3b98af7.jpg;The journey to the top of Schafberg is an experience in itself. The historic Schafberg Railway slowly climbs the steep mountainside, offering breathtaking views over lakes and valleys along the way. At the summit—often referred to as “Himmelsport” for its heavenly vistas—visitors are rewarded with sweeping panoramas of the Salzkammergut’s lakes and the distant Alps. It’s a place where the sky feels closer, and the silence of the mountains creates a truly unforgettable alpine moment.|https://www.bergwelten.com/files/hut/images/himmelspforte-klaus-brenner-fotowettbewerb18-bergwelten.jpg
goBeyondText: A visit to Salzburg from St. Wolfgang is an easy and rewarding day trip. Known as the birthplace of Mozart and the setting for The Sound of Music, Salzburg offers a rich blend of culture and history. Its baroque old town, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is filled with elegant squares, churches, and cafés. Walking through its streets or along the Salzach River provides a lively contrast to the शांत, nature-filled atmosphere of the lake region.

name: Mdina
description: Step into silent streets, golden stone and timeless medieval charm
